financial_fit.justification: €78M revenue and 7.2% EBIT margin place Bavaria Motors squarely in the KUKA sweet spot for mid-market automotive suppliers with healthy margins and capex capacity.

headcount_fit.justification: 220 employees is ideal for KUKA's Mittelstand ICP — large enough to justify multi-cell deployments, small enough to lack in-house robotics expertise.

industry_automation_intensity.justification: Automotive Tier 1 powertrain components is KUKA's heartland; arc welding, machine loading, and assembly are proven applications with off-the-shelf cells.

look_alike.justification: Same region (Bavaria), same industry (automotive Tier 1), family-owned, similar headcount. Schneider runs 12 KUKA robots and is a willing reference.

stakeholder_identifiability.justification: VP Production is the textbook entry point for KUKA manufacturing automation. Dr. Kurz is new in role (Jan 2026 from ZF) and likely looking for early wins.

contact_completeness.justification: Direct email and phone confirmed. LinkedIn profile active. Three alternates identified including family owner.

trigger_events.justification: Four concurrent triggers: BMW i-series contract (Feb 2026), modernisation programme (Mar 2026), new VP Production (Jan 2026), and acute welder shortage.

headcount_fit.justification: 130 employees fits the KUKA mid-market sweet spot — enough volume to justify automation, manual enough to show dramatic ROI.

industry_automation_intensity.justification: EMS/PCB assembly is a high-automation-intensity sector; pick-and-place, soldering, inspection, and testing are prime KUKA cobot applications.

look_alike.justification: Novara Technologies is a near-identical match: electronics assembly, similar scale, PE-backed, running KUKA cobots for PCB handling and testing.

contact_completeness.justification: Direct email, phone, and LinkedIn confirmed. Four alternates identified including CEO, CTO, and CFO.

trigger_events.justification: Four strong triggers: new VP Ops from Foxconn (Dec 2025), €5M Industry 4.0 investment (Mar 2026), Bosch ECU contract with <0.3% defect requirement (Jan 2026), PE demanding 60% EBITDA improvement.

headcount_fit.justification: 175 employees is well within KUKA's manufacturing ICP; large enough for multi-robot deployments across the new CNC hall.

industry_automation_intensity.justification: Aerospace structural parts require CNC machine loading, part handling, and deburring — all proven KUKA applications with strict traceability requirements that favour integrated solutions.

look_alike.justification: Hartmann Metallbau is the closest match — heavy CNC machining, 15 KUKA robots, lighthouse customer. Slightly different end-market (general engineering vs. aerospace) lowers similarity.

headcount_fit.justification: 145 employees is within the ICP but pharma's lower robot-density-per-employee means the addressable automation footprint is slightly smaller than an equivalent automotive firm.

industry_automation_intensity.justification: Pharmaceutical packaging, serialisation, and cleanroom handling are high-automation-intensity applications where KUKA's cleanroom-rated robots (KR AGILUS, LBR iisy) have strong positioning.

look_alike.justification: Bayer Pharma-Pack is an excellent match: same region (NRW), same industry (pharma packaging), GMP-compliant, running 6 KUKA robots in cleanroom environments.

look_alike.justification: Hartmann Metallbau is a strong match: same industry (metal fabrication), similar scale originally, now 15 KUKA robots. Provides a clear growth trajectory reference.

industry_automation_intensity.justification: Food packaging and palletising are proven KUKA applications; hygienic design robots (IP67, washdown-rated) are available but the sector is less robotics-dense than automotive.

look_alike.justification: No direct food customer anchor. Bayer Pharma-Pack is the closest in terms of hygienic environment handling, but different industry. KUKA food & beverage case studies partially bridge the gap.
